steps.title: Find the GCF of the two terms
steps.content:
The expression $6x^8y^2 + 12x^2y^2$ has two terms. To factor, identify the greatest common factor:
  • Coefficients: GCF(6, 12) = 6
  • Powers of $x$: GCF($x^8$, $x^2$) = $x^2$
  • Powers of $y$: GCF($y^2$, $y^2$) = $y^2$
The overall GCF is $6x^2y^2$.

steps.title: Factor and match to a choice
steps.content:
Divide each term by the GCF $6x^2y^2$:
$$\frac{6x^8y^2}{6x^2y^2} = x^6$$
$$\frac{12x^2y^2}{6x^2y^2} = 2$$
The factored form is:
$$6x^2y^2(x^6 + 2)$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
